Article Title: 'He beats us even in socks': Amateur players can't believe as World Cup star Michael Olise joins random New York kickabout
Article Text:
France's Michael Olise holds the ball during the World Cup round of 16 soccer match between Paraguay and France in Philadelphia, Saturday, July 4, 2026. (AP Photo/Derik Hamilton)

Socks, oversized trousers and trademark quality

Strong World Cup despite missing out on a goal

Real Madrid links continue

Bayern's Michael Olise, right, talks to Bayern's Harry Kane during the Champions League opening phase soccer match between FC Bayern Munich and Sporting CP in Munich, Germany, Tuesday, Dec. 9, 2025. (AP Photo/Matthias Schrader)

Rapid rise in Munich

Just days after helping France finish fourth at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Bayern Munich winger Michael Olise traded football's biggest stage for a casual kickabout in New York City, leaving a group of amateur players stunned after unexpectedly joining their match.The France international had featured throughout the World Cup in North America, where Les Bleus narrowly missed out on the bronze medal after suffering a dramatic 6-4 defeat to England in the third-place play-off. Despite the disappointment, Olise appeared unwilling to stay away from football for long.Instead of immediately heading home following the tournament, the 24-year-old was spotted taking part in a recreational game organised through GoodRec, a platform that connects amateur footballers looking to play matches in cities across the world.Videos shared on social media quickly went viral, showing the Bayern star lining up alongside everyday players on a public pitch in New York.Perhaps the most unusual aspect of Olise's appearance was not that he was playing in a public game, but how he was dressed.The Bayern attacker took to the field wearing socks instead of football boots and loose, chequered trousers that repeatedly slipped beneath his feet as he ran.Even so, flashes of his quality were immediately evident.One clip captured Olise cutting in from the right onto his favoured left foot, one of his trademark moves, but an opponent managed to block the effort before it could find the far corner.For the players sharing the pitch with him, it was an experience few could have imagined.GoodRec later posted a group photograph featuring Olise standing among both teams, maintaining his typically reserved expression after the game."He beats us even in socks," the platform wrote on social media. "Thank you for coming to a GoodRec game in New York City!"The Bayern forward also posed for photographs with everyone involved, adding another memorable moment to an evening that had begun as nothing more than a routine amateur football match.Olise arrived in New York after an impressive World Cup campaign with France.Although he failed to score during the tournament, the creative winger played a crucial role in Didier Deschamps' side, registering seven assists across eight appearances. His creativity made him one of the competition's standout playmakers, even as France eventually finished fourth following their defeat to England in Miami.His performances also earned him a place in FIFA's official Dream XI for the tournament, selected through fan voting, alongside the likes of Spain captain Rodri, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland.Olise's World Cup performances have only intensified speculation surrounding his future.Reports in Germany have continued to link the French international with Real Madrid, while Sport Bild has claimed France captain Kylian Mbappé attempted during the World Cup to convince his international teammate that a move to the Santiago Bernabéu would be an attractive option.However, Bayern Munich have repeatedly insisted they have no intention of selling one of their most influential players this summer.According to reports in Germany, Real Madrid have since accepted that persuading Bayern to part with Olise during the current transfer window is highly unlikely.The French winger remains under contract with the Bundesliga champions until 2029.Since arriving from Crystal Palace in the summer of 2024, Olise has developed into one of Europe's most productive attacking players.Across all competitions last season, he produced an outstanding return of 22 goals and 31 assists in 52 appearances for Bayern, establishing himself as one of the club's most important players.His spontaneous appearance on a public pitch in New York therefore came as a surprise to many, but it also reinforced the reputation Olise has built throughout his career—a footballer who simply enjoys playing the game, whether in front of tens of thousands at a World Cup or alongside local players on a community pitch just days after the tournament had ended.
Article Summary: Bayern munich's michael olise played in the 2026 fifa world cup. The 24-year-old helped france finish fourth in north america. Olise was spotted playing in a recreational game in new york city. The bayern winger wore socks and loose, chequered trousers. Bayern munich winger olise has been linked with a move to real madrid.

Article Title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi announces international retirement
Article Text:
Argentina's Nicolas Otamendi (19) (AP Photo/Ed Zurga)

Decorated career comes to an end

Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has announced his retirement from international football, ending a remarkable 17-year career with the national team.The 38-year-old leaves as one of Argentina's most successful defenders, having won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final appearance came in Argentina's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final in the United States.Otamendi made 139 appearances for Argentina and was a key figure in the team's success over the years. After the final, he shared an emotional message on Instagram, thanking the country for giving him the chance to achieve his biggest dream."Today I have to write the most difficult words of my career. As fate would have it, my last match was a World Cup final. It wasn't the result we wanted, but I'm leaving with my head held high, knowing that this team gave it their all right up to the very last second. Thank you, Argentina, for allowing me to fulfil the dream of becoming a world champion," he wrote.Argentina's hopes of defending their World Cup title ended with a narrow extra-time loss to Spain in the final.The team had shown great fighting spirit throughout the tournament, recovering from two goals down to beat Egypt in the Round of 32 before staging another dramatic comeback against England in the semi-finals.However, Spain proved too strong in the final and ended Argentina's reign as world champions.Otamendi also recently returned to Argentina to join River Plate after a successful club career in Europe. During his time abroad, he played for Porto, Valencia, Manchester City and Benfica, winning several major honours.Having represented Argentina at four FIFA World Cups, Otamendi retires as one of the country's most experienced and accomplished defenders, leaving behind a legacy built on consistency, leadership and success at the highest level.
Article Summary: Nicolas otamendi has announced his retirement from international football. The 38-year-old defender made 139 appearances for argentina. His final appearance came in argentina's 1-0 defeat to spain in the fifa world cup final in the united states. After the final he shared an emotional message on instagram.

Article Title: How to pre-order EA Sports FC 27: price, editions, bonus and more
Article Text:
EA FC 27 is coming out in September (Image via EA)

All EA Sports FC 27 editions and pricing

Standard Edition - $69.99

Ultimate Edition - $99.99

Ultimate Plus Edition - $149.99

How to pre-order EA Sports FC 27

On PC (Steam)

Go to the EA Sports FC 27 Steam store page, select the edition you want, and complete the payment.

On PlayStation 5 and PS4

Navigate to the EA Sports FC 27 store page on the PlayStation Store, find the edition you want, tap on the Pre-order option, and complete the transaction.

On Xbox Series X|S

Go to the EA Sports FC 27 page on the Xbox Store, choose your preferred edition, tap the green Pre-Order button and complete purchase.

Which EA FC 27 edition should you get?

EA Sports FC 27 is officially on the way, and pre-orders are now live across all major platforms. EA has unveiled the launch trailer, and Kylian Mbappe is on the cover this time around.Essentially, EA FC 27 is set to release on September 25, 2026, and the game features three editions: Standard, Ultimate, and Ultimate Plus. Here is a complete breakdown of everything you need to know before putting your money down.EA Sports FC 27 has three editions: Standard, Ultimate, and Ultimate Plus. The Standard Edition is priced at $69.99, the Ultimate Edition at $99.99, and the Ultimate Plus Edition at $149.99.The entry-level edition gets you the base game alongside a solid selection of pre-order bonuses. It comes with a 92+ OVR ICON Player Item for EA Sports FC 26, The Grounds Signature Sweatsuit, a Tier 3 Amp inspired by Mbappe, 2 Double AXP for 10 Matches Consumables, Pre-purchase Challenges, 3 ICONs/Heroes, a 5-Star Coach, and a 5-Star Youth Scout available for hire in Manager Career.The Ultimate Edition comes with all the rewards from the Standard Edition, plus up to 7 days of Advanced Access, a Season 1 Premium Pass, a FUT Player Dribbling Evo for FC 26, a Career Dynamic OVR Pre-season Boost, 6,000 FC Points over 3 months, a FUT Player Evo slot, 5 Manager Live Challenge Creation Slots, and a 5-Star Senior Scout in Manager Career.There is also a 10% discount running on this edition until launch. Two limited-time pre-purchase rewards apply for pre-orders before August 31 — an 85+ OVR International ICON Player Item and a 93+ OVR Icon Player Pick for FC 26.The Ultimate Plus Edition comes with all rewards from the Standard and Ultimate Editions, plus limited-time exclusive rewards only available before August 31 — including Seasons 1 to 5 Premium Passes, 10,000 FC Points over 5 drops, an 85+ OVR International ICON Player Item, a Hall of FUT Player Pick, and an additional FUT Player Evo slot. Core rewards include a 93+ OVR ICON Player Pick, a FUT Player Dribbling stats Evo, and a FUT Player Physical stats Evo.Standard Edition at $69.99 is great value for most of the regular users who want to experience EA FC 27. Ultimate Edition could turn out to be a worthy upgrade if you want early access and the seasonal FC Points allowance.Finally, the Ultimate Plus Edition is designed for the most dedicated FUT players, with the highest amount of player picks and all five season passes from launch.
Article Summary: Ea sports fc 27 is set to release on september 25, 2026. There are three editions: standard, ultimate, and ultimate plus. Standard edition is priced at $69.99, the ultimate edition at $99.99 and the ultimate plus edition is $149.99. Pre-orders are now live across all major platforms.

Article Title: Lovlina Borgohain assured of CWG medal after direct semis entry
Article Text:
Lovlina Borgohain (PTI Photo)

NEW DELHI: Lovlina Borgohain became India’s first assured medallist at the Commonwealth Games (CWG) even before stepping into the ring after receiving a direct entry into the women’s 75kg semifinals, guaranteeing herself at least a bronze medal.With only five entries in the 75kg division, the quarterfinal stage effectively became a formality. Three boxers — Lovlina, Tuvalu’s TKBP Taafaki and Australia’s Emma-Sue Greentree — received first-round byes and advanced straight to the semifinals.In boxing, both losing semifinalists are awarded bronze medals, ensuring the Tokyo Olympic medallist a place on the podium without contesting a bout. Lovlina will face Taafaki in the semifinal on July 31, with the winner progressing to the gold medal bout. Should she meet Greentree in the final, the Australian is expected to provide a stern challenge.The guaranteed bronze also completes Lovlina’s collection of major multisport medals. The 28-year-old Assam boxer had previously won medals at the Olympics, World Championships, Asian Games and Asian Championships, with a CWG medal being the only honour missing from her cabinet.The sparse field in the women’s 75kg category stands in sharp contrast to the men’s events, where participation is significantly stronger.The men’s 55kg division (Jadumani Singh’s category) has the largest field with 24 boxers, while most men’s weight classes have healthy entries, barring the 90kg and 90+kg, which feature fewer than 10 competitors.Among women, the 54kg division ( Preeti Pawar ) has the biggest draw with 17 entrants, followed by the 51kg category (Sakshi Chaudhary) with 13.The boxing competition gets underway from Friday with Jadumani in action on the first day.
Article Summary: Lovlina borgohain becomes india's first assured medallist at the commonwealth games. The 28-year-old assam boxer will face tuvalu’s tkbp taafaki in the semifinal on july 31. The winner progresses to the gold medal bout.

Article Title: Best of best: FIFA unveils World Cup Dream XI with 11 players voted as tournament's finest
Article Text:
FIFA announce star-studded World Cup Dream XI featuring Messi, Mbappé and Haaland/ Image: X

Bellingham flies England's flag





Spain, France and Argentina dominate selections





Cape Verde cult hero earns goalkeeper spot

FIFA World Cup 2026 Dream XI





Goalkeeper

Vozinha (Cape Verde)

Defenders

Pedro Porro (Spain)

Lisandro Martínez (Argentina)

Dayot Upamecano (France)

Marc Cucurella (Spain)

Midfielders

Rodri (Spain)

Michael Olise (France)

Jude Bellingham (England)

Forwards

Lionel Messi (Argentina)

Erling Haaland (Norway)

Kylian Mbappé (France)

Spain may have lifted the 2026 FIFA World Cup trophy after defeating Argentina 1-0 following extra time in New Jersey, but the tournament's best-performing players have now also been recognised after FIFA revealed its official Dream XI.The Team of the Tournament, selected through a global fan vote, reflects the standout performers from six weeks of football across the United States, Canada and Mexico. While Spain, France and Argentina dominate the selection after reaching the semi-finals, England's third-place finish was rewarded with just a single representative.Jude Bellingham is the only England player to make FIFA's Dream XI despite Thomas Tuchel's side ending the tournament on a high with a remarkable 6-4 victory over France in the third-place play-off.Bellingham's inclusion comes after another outstanding major tournament for the Real Madrid midfielder.The 23-year-old finished with seven goals, the highest tally ever recorded by an England player at a single FIFA World Cup, helping the Three Lions secure their best finish since winning the tournament in 1966.Despite England producing several standout performers, including Bukayo Saka's historic hat-trick against France and Declan Rice's record-breaking third-place play-off display, Bellingham was the only English player voted into the final XI.Fresh from captaining Spain to their second World Cup title, Rodri headlines the midfield after also winning the Golden Ball as the tournament's best player.Spain have three representatives in total, with both full-backs Marc Cucurella and Pedro Porro joining their captain in the side after Luis de la Fuente's men completed an unbeaten tournament by defeating Argentina in the final.France also supply three players despite finishing fourth.Golden Boot winner Kylian Mbappé occupies the right side of the attack after finishing the tournament with 10 goals, becoming the first player to win the World Cup Golden Boot twice while also breaking Lionel Messi's all-time World Cup scoring record with 22 career goals.Michael Olise takes one of the midfield places after producing a record-breaking campaign of his own. The France playmaker finished the tournament with seven assists, the most ever recorded by a player at a single FIFA World Cup.Dayot Upamecano completes France's contingent in central defence.Argentina, beaten finalists after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner, have two players in the XI.Lionel Messi features on the left wing after finishing the tournament with eight goals and four assists, while defender Lisandro Martínez partners Upamecano at the heart of the defence.Completing the front three is Norway striker Erling Haaland, who marked his World Cup debut by firing his country to a historic first-ever quarter-final appearance while scoring seven goals during the tournament.Perhaps the biggest surprise in the Dream XI is the goalkeeper selection.Rather than Golden Glove winner Unai Simón, England goalkeeper Jordan Pickford or France's Mike Maignan, fans voted for Cape Verde veteran Vozinha.The 40-year-old became one of the breakout stars of the tournament during Cape Verde's memorable run to the Round of 32 before they narrowly lost 3-2 after extra time to eventual finalists Argentina.Vozinha kept clean sheets against both Spain and Saudi Arabia, made 18 saves across the tournament and outperformed his expected goals against figure by 0.9.His performances also made him an unlikely social media sensation. According to tournament figures, the goalkeeper began Cape Verde's opening match against Spain with around 50,000 Instagram followers. After producing seven saves against the eventual champions and continuing his impressive campaign, that number reportedly surged to 29.5 million by the end of the World Cup.The Dream XI rounds off the individual honours following the conclusion of the tournament.Rodri claimed the Golden Ball as the competition's best player after leading Spain to the title, with Messi and Mbappé receiving the Silver Ball and Bronze Ball respectively.Mbappé finished as Golden Boot winner with 10 goals, edging Messi, who ended the tournament on eight goals and four assists, while Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón received the Golden Glove after conceding just one goal in eight matches. Teenage defender Pau Cubarsí collected the FIFA Young Player Award, and the Netherlands were recognised with the FIFA Fair Play Award after receiving just three yellow cards throughout their campaign.
Article Summary: Fifa announce star-studded world cup dream xi featuring messi, mbappé and haaland. Spain, france and argentina dominate the selection after reaching the semi-finals. England's third-place finish was rewarded with just a single representative. Rodri claimed the golden ball as the competition's best player after leading spain to the title.

Article Title: Father's 'Govardhan Parikrama' in Mathura pays off as Mayank Yadav makes a fiery return
Article Text:
India's bowler Mayank Yadav gestures as he prepares to bowl during the first T20 cricket match between Zimbabwe and India in Harare, Zimbabwe, Thursday, July 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Wonder Mashura)

New Delhi:

Kaise daala? Maine toh match dekha tak nahi. Mai toh bas kal raat hi Mathura aa gaya tha, bas abhi

Govardhan Bhagwaan

ki parikrama kar ke waapis nikal raha hun

Bas fit rahe, yahi maanga hai. Pichle do saal kaafi mushkil gaye hai bachhe ke

Kyu mazak kar rahe ho sir?' (Are you kidding me, sir?) Those were his words when I told him about his selection. He was not expecting a call Devender Sharma, coach of Mayank Yadav

Mayank Yadav (Pic credit: BCCI)

Kyu mazak kar rahe ho sir

At the CoE, for the past year he has been bowling close to 15 overs every day. The aim is to bowl 20 overs a day. They want him to be ready for Test cricket from the next World Test Championship (WTC) cycle Devender Sharma | Mayank Yadav's coach

Mayank Yadav celebrates a wicket

First it was a stress fracture in the back, then an ankle injury, followed by calf and knee injuries. It is not easy to cope with so many setbacks Narinder Negi | Mayank Yadav's mentor

Mullanpur: India's bowling coach Morne Morkel, right, interacts with Mayank Yadav during a practice session ahead of a Test cricket match against Afghanistan, in Mullanpur. (PTI Photo/Atul Yadav) (PTI06_05_2026_000020B)

Prabhu Yadav was performing the 'Govardhan Parikrama' in Mathura when his son, Mayank Yadav, over 7,000 kms away at the Harare Sports Club, was bowling thunderbolts. The 24-year-old pacer, making a comeback after two years, returned with a sensational spell of 2 for 18. It included a 149 kph first-ball wicket in the first T20I against Zimbabwe, dismissing Brian Bennett , who had given India a scare with a stunning 97 during the T20 World Cup earlier this year.(How did he bowl? I didn't even watch the match. I only came to Mathura last night. I've just finished the Govardhan Bhagwan Parikrama and am now heading back)," he tells TimesofIndia.com from Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, after completing the sacred 21 km circumambulation around Govardhan Hill.(I have prayed for his fitness and success. The last two years have been really tough on the boy)," says Yadav Sr., gasping for breath after the long walk.While Prabhu Yadav, who works at a factory in Okhla manufacturing sirens for ambulances and police vehicles, struggled for breath after a tiring seven-hour walk, Yadav Jr. made Zimbabwe batters dance to his tunes during a hostile spell.It has been a tough couple of years for Mayank Yadav. After impressing everyone with his express pace during IPL 2024, he was sidelined for over a year due to a stress fracture. But the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) never lost faith in the youngster. For the past two years, the BCCI's Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Bengaluru became his second home."It was not easy for the boy. The frequent injuries took a toll on him physically and mentally," shares Devender Sharma, who coached the speedster during his formative years."He was upset. But he worked hard. Every single day, he gave his best. After any injury, rehabilitation becomes very important, and he completed it diligently. That has helped him get back where he belongs," says Sharma.Devender recalls how Mayank was in shock when he congratulated him over the phone after he was picked for the three-match series against Zimbabwe."'?' (Are you kidding me, sir?) Those were his words when I told him about his selection. He was not expecting a call," shares Devender.Mayank's much-anticipated IPL return did not go according to plan this year; he failed to take a single wicket in the four games he played for the Lucknow Super Giants (LSG), conceding 182 runs at an economy rate of 11.37.However, he was always in the scheme of things. He was named in the India A squad for the warm-up matches against the USA and Namibia before the T20 World Cup. Thereafter, he was among the seven net bowlers summoned by Team India to New Chandigarh ahead of the one-off Test against Afghanistan. Throughout the three net sessions, Mayank bowled at rapid pace with the red ball and was closely watched by bowling coach Morne Morkel."Look, he is a special talent, and that's why you see the BCCI backed him. They never got frustrated. They showed faith in him," says Sharma.Devender also shared what the youngster has been doing over the past year."At the CoE, for the past year he has been bowling close to 15 overs every day. The aim is to bowl 20 overs a day. They want him to be ready for Test cricket from the next World Test Championship (WTC) cycle. He has been managed very carefully by all the stakeholders, and I hope he stays fit and repays the faith that the board has shown in him," says Sharma.Mayank's mentor and former Delhi U-19 coach Narender Negi echoes Devender's sentiments and shares details about the bowler's injury struggles."It is not easy to clock 145 kph consistently. He got unlucky with injuries as well. First it was a stress fracture in the back, then an ankle injury, followed by calf and knee injuries. It is not easy to cope with so many setbacks," says Negi."People wrote him off and I think today he proved everyone wrong. Yes, it is just one match, but he is back for sure," adds Negi.On Thursday, Mayank consistently operated in the mid-to-high 140s. He troubled Zimbabwe's batters with sharp seam movement, steep bounce and relentless pace. During the innings break, he admitted he was nervous before bowling his first ball."It's always a great feeling when you come back and play for your country, especially when you bowl a spell like this. It's a fantastic feeling and I don't think I can describe it well in words. Obviously there were some nerves. To make a comeback after two years, there is always going to be pressure. There was some nervousness in the middle," he told the broadcasters."After picking up a wicket on the very first ball, I calmed down a lot. I got the confidence that I can deliver even after making a comeback after two years," he added.It has been a tumultuous couple of years for the Delhi pacer. As his father says, he has hardly been home, while his mother hopes her son will finally spend some time with the family after this series."Ab shayad kuch din ghar rahe (Hopefully, he will stay at home for a few days now)," says Mamta Yadav, Mayank's mother.For Mayank, the wickets in Harare were more than just numbers on a scorecard, it was the speedgun which he must have enjoyed. It marked the end of a long, painful road filled with injuries, rehabilitation and uncertainty. With no compromise on pace, the Delhi quick has finally given himself the perfect platform to chase the promise that first made him one of India's most exciting fast-bowling prospects.
Article Summary: Mayank yadav made a comeback after two years with a sensational spell of 2 for 18. It included a 149 kph first-ball wicket in the first t20i against zimbabwe. The 24-year-old pacer was sidelined for over a year due to a stress fracture. He has been bowling close to 15 overs every day for the past year.

Article Title: Jos Buttler scripts history, surpasses Chris Gayle on all-time T20 run charts
Article Text:
England's Jos Buttler (ANI Photo)

Most runs in T20 cricket:

Kieron Pollard (West Indies) – 14,803

Jos Buttler (England) – 14,572

Chris Gayle (West Indies) – 14,562

Alex Hales (England) – 14,449

(England) – 14,449 David Warner (Australia) – 14,284

Virat Kohli (India) – 14,218

England wicketkeeper-batter Jos Buttler etched his name further into the record books on Thursday, surpassing Chris Gayle to climb to second on the all-time T20 run-scoring charts during The Hundred 2026 clash between Manchester Super Giants and London Spirit at Lord's.The landmark came despite Buttler making only 16 off 11 balls, an innings that included one four and one six. Those runs were enough to move him past Gayle and underline the remarkable turnaround in fortunes after a difficult spell over the past 18 months.Buttler now has 14,572 runs from 487 T20 innings at a strike rate of 146.74. His tally includes 102 half-centuries and nine hundreds.Gayle, who held the all-time T20 run-scoring record for years before being overtaken by Kieron Pollard earlier this year, has now dropped another place to third with 14,562 runs from 455 innings. The former West Indies opener finished his career with a strike rate of 144.75, while registering 88 fifties and 22 centuries.West Indies all-rounder Kieron Pollard continues to lead the all-time list with 14,803 runs from 663 innings at a strike rate of 151.02, having scored 70 fifties and two centuries. Buttler is now just 231 runs away from claiming the top spot.The milestone marks another chapter in Buttler's impressive return to form after enduring one of the toughest periods of his career.The England star struggled badly over the previous year and a half as runs became increasingly difficult to come by.His lean run extended to the ICC Men's T20 World Cup, where he endured a forgettable campaign, scoring only 87 runs in eight innings at an average of 10.67.However, Buttler has rediscovered his touch in 2026. Representing Gujarat Titans in the IPL, he amassed 526 runs at a strike rate of 152.46, re-establishing himself as one of the tournament's most dangerous batters.He carried that form into the international arena as well, producing a stunning unbeaten 131 off just 64 balls against India in the final T20I of the series. The knock stands as the highest individual score ever recorded against India in a men's T20I.Buttler has also built an outstanding T20I career for England. Across 160 matches, he has accumulated 4,212 runs at a strike rate of 148.93, registering 28 half-centuries and two centuries.He currently sits third on the all-time men's T20I run-scorers list, behind Pakistan's Babar Azam (4,596) and India's Rohit Sharma (4,231).The 35-year-old is comfortably England's leading run-scorer in T20 internationals and remains the only English batter to cross both the 3,000 and 4,000-run marks in the format. Former captain Eoin Morgan is a distant second with 2,458 runs from 115 T20Is.
Article Summary: Buttler now has 14,572 runs from 487 t20 innings at a strike rate of 146.74. His tally includes 102 half-centuries and nine hundreds. West indies all-rounder kieron pollard continues to lead the all-time list.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's maiden T20I fifty seals emphatic India victory
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Showing the kind of murderous touch that has made him the most feared batsman in the IPL, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi blazed to his maiden international half-century as India thrashed Zimbabwe by seven wickets, with 40 balls to spare, in the opening T20I at the Harare Sports Club on Thursday.Aged 15 years and 118 days, Sooryavanshi smashed 50 off only 18 balls in an innings studded with four sixes and four fours.The teenage batting sensation had endured a difficult start to his international career during India's T20I series against England in July, after scores of 14 (10), 13 (5) and 15 (10) in his first three innings.Returning to the scene of his 80-ball 175 against England in the 2026 U-19 World Cup final, the precociously talented opener produced a dazzling display to make light work of a modest 126-run chase, racing to an 18-ball fifty.Sooryavanshi smashed 19 runs off Richard Ngarava in the second over, clobbering the left-arm pacer for two sixes.Zimbabwe hit back in the third over as Blessing Muzarabani (2-26) had Abhishek Sharma , who scratched around for one off eight balls, caught at deep backward point off a slash. But Sooryavanshi continued to attack, launching two more sixes straight down the ground besides several fiercely struck boundaries.He eventually fell to Ngarava in the seventh over, chasing a wide delivery outside off and top-edging to Ben Curran at deep third.By then, however, India had raced to 68 and the youngster had virtually settled the contest.The visitors completed the chase in just 13.2 overs. The win also ended new T20 captain Shreyas Iyer 's winless run, as he registered his maiden victory in his eighth match in charge. Shreyas had lost six of his first seven games as captain during the tours of Ireland and England.It was also India's first win to kick off the three-match series since lifting the 2026 T20 World Cup in March.Earlier, India's new-look pace attack starred after the visitors elected to field, with Mayank Yadav (2-18) and Prince Yadav (2-19) sharing four wickets for just 37 runs in eight overs to restrict Zimbabwe to 125 for seven.Vindicating the selectors' decision to back him despite an ordinary IPL 2026, Mayank made an immediate impact by dismissing opener Brian Bennett, who had scored 97 against India in the 2026 T20 World Cup in Chennai, with the very first ball of the match.His 150 kmph thunderbolt brushed Bennett's bat before carrying through to wicketkeeper Ishan Kishan, whose review overturned the umpire's decision. Mayank struck again in the fifth over, removing No. 3 batter Dion Myers for six, caught at mid-on.Prince accounted for the other opener, debutant Ben Curran, as Zimbabwe slumped to 20 for three. The hosts slipped further to 32 for four in the eighth over when captain Sikandar Raza (4) pulled Shivam Dube straight to substitute fielder Suryansh Shedge at deep midwicket.Brief scores: India 126 for 3 (Sooryavanshi 50, Kishan 35, Muzarabani 2-26) beat Zimbabwe 125 for 7 (Madhevere 39, Mayank 2-18, Prince 2-19) by seven wickets.
Article Summary: India beat zimbabwe by seven wickets in the opening t20i at harare sports club. Sooryavanshi smashed 50 off only 18 balls in an innings studded with four sixes and four fours. The teenage batting sensation had endured a difficult start to his international career.

Article Title: Ashes in India? Cricket Australia leaves door open for landmark 150th Test
Article Text:
Steve Smith, left, and Ben Stokes pose for a photo with Ashes Trophy (AP photo)

Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg has said the board would be open to exploring the possibility of staging the landmark 150th anniversary Ashes Test in India, underlining the growing importance of the Indian market to world cricket.Australia and England are scheduled to play a one-off Test in March next year to commemorate 150 years of the historic Ashes rivalry. While there are no current plans to take the match outside Australia, Greenberg admitted the idea of hosting it in India could be considered in the future.His comments come at a time when Cricket Australia is expanding its footprint in India. This December, the Big Bash League will stage a match in Chennai, where Melbourne Renegades will face Perth Scorchers, marking another step in bringing Australian cricket closer to Indian fans.The prospect of an Ashes Test in India also reflects the commercial significance of the country's cricket market. India remains the sport's biggest audience, and hosting a marquee contest between Australia and England would generate enormous interest while creating fresh commercial opportunities.Greenberg said Cricket Australia would not rule out the idea, citing its strong ties with both the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) and the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI)."We'd have to be open to consider all options," Greenberg told the BBC's Stumped podcast. "Our relationship with the ECB, but also the BCCI, is very strong. And clearly, there are opportunities in India to put more content into certain areas. That's not something we are currently planning, but it would certainly be open for consideration."India's influence on the global cricket economy has continued to grow in recent years. The ECB, for instance, is expected to post substantial profits from the eight white-ball internationals it hosted against India recently, despite England winning six of those matches. The financial returns are expected to surpass what the board anticipates from staging both the men's and women's Ashes series next year.Despite his openness to the proposal, Greenberg acknowledged that scheduling a five-day Ashes Test in India would be far from straightforward.The Indian calendar is already packed with international cricket and the IPL, which is expected to begin earlier than usual next year to avoid the extreme heat towards the end of May. Finding a suitable window for another marquee event would therefore be a major challenge."I think Test cricket between us, England and India, is still very strong, probably bucking the trend as to other parts of the world. So we want to make sure we continue to grow it and strengthen it."The challenge for all of us in global cricket is there are only 365 days in a schedule, and we probably need another month or two if we actually can generate the content we want to," he said.
Article Summary: Cricket australia chief executive todd greenberg says the board would be open to exploring the possibility of staging the landmark 150th anniversary ashes test in india. Australia and england are scheduled to play a one-off test in march next year to commemorate 150 years of the historic ashes rivalry.

Article Title: What did Sophie Cunningham say about trans athlete in women’s sport? New controversy surrounds Fever guard
Article Text:
Sophie Cunningham. Image via: USA TODAY Network via Reuters Connect

What did Sophie Cunningham say about trans athletes?

WNBA and teammates have weighed in before

Sophie Cunningham previously commented on trans athletes as well

Sometimes, it’s her viral meme, or her stunning appearance, sometimes her on court feuds or her blunt commments; Sophie Cunningham always keep her name in the biggest WNBA discussions. Sophie Cunningham is once again at the center of the trans athlete debate after ESPN's Hallie Grossman published a profile on the Indiana Fever guard July 21 that dug into her views on transgender participation in women's sports. Cunningham told ESPN she has been mischaracterized as anti-trans, saying instead that her goal is protecting girls and young women in athletics from competing against those she describes as biological men.The comments arrive as Cunningham, one of the WNBA's most recognizable and polarizing players, continues to push back on the "MAGA Barbie" label attached to her. They also land during a season in which the Fever's locker room has been drawn repeatedly into national political fights, largely tied to the treatment of teammate Caitlin Clark.Cunningham told ESPN, "I got a lot of negative feedback about me hating trans."She went on to frame her position as one rooted in protecting young female athletes from facing what she called biological men in competition, while insisting she isn't trying to alienate anyone."I think that I am here to extend love. But I also think with that love is truth, being honest. And I want to protect young girls in a locker room, or young girls in sport who shouldn't have to go against biological men."On her broader political identity, she pushed back against being boxed in, saying she holds views from both sides of the aisle and resents assumptions made about her based on her image and background.Grossman's profile noted that Cunningham feels qualified to weigh in on the subject because of her own background as a young female athlete. The Missouri native, now in her second season with Indiana, is averaging 9.4 points per game on 48 percent shooting.The debate is not new to the Fever roster. Former Cunningham teammate Brianna Turner, now with the Las Vegas Aces, wrote an op-ed for USA Today earlier this year after the International Olympic Committee barred trans athletes from competing in their chosen gender category. Turner argued the conversation "distracts from the structural inequities that actually limit women's opportunities."The WNBA players' association has also gone on record in support of trans inclusion, stating in a 2022 release that banning trans kids from sports "ostracizes them from a fundamental part of growing up." The Fever organization had not commented on Cunningham's remarks as of publication.This isn't the first time Cunningham has waded into the topic. In 2022, she shared a since-deleted tweet from CBS Sports host Josh Pate, who wrote that "Female collegiate athletes deserve so much better than this" regarding transgender swimmer Lia Thomas' run on Penn's women's team. Cunningham followed with her own statement on X, saying she stands with "all people, especially my fellow athletes," and voicing support for people of all backgrounds competing fairly.Indiana's own scholastic and collegiate transgender athlete ban was upheld by the Supreme Court in late June, adding another layer to the debate playing out around Cunningham's team. Whether the Fever organization issues a formal response, or whether Cunningham's comments follow her into the All-Star break, remains an open question as Indiana closes out the first half of its season.
Article Summary: Sophie cunningham is one of the wnba's most recognizable and polarizing players. Espn published a profile on the indiana fever guard that dug into her views on transgender participation in women's sports. Cunningham told espn she has been mischaracterized as anti-trans, saying her goal is protecting girls and young women in athletics.

Article Title: “One more chip in you”: Stephen Curry’s wife Ayesha Curry makes candid confession about how she inspires Warriors legend for another title run
Article Text:
Stephen Curry’ and Ayesha Curry. Image via: Taylor Hill/Getty Images

Ayesha Curry reveals how she keeps Stephen Curry inspired

Ayesha Curry to star in a romantic comedy

Ayesha Curry celebrated Stephen Curry's ESPYs win

Last season, Stephen Curry was riddled with injuries in the final moments of the Warriors' run, but he’s not done. Ayesha Curry says she has one message for her husband heading into another Golden State Warriors season: one more championship is still in him. The actress and entrepreneur shared that sentiment in a joint interview with The Hollywood Reporter, days after Stephen picked up the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award at the 2026 ESPYs for his work with the Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation.The comments land in the middle of a packed summer for the couple. Stephen is rehabbing from last season's knee injury and weighing a contract extension, while Ayesha steps into her first lead film role in Roku's holiday rom-com. Together, the pair are also set to be named Philanthropists of the Year at The Hollywood Reporter's Social Impact Summit.Stephen missed 27 straight games last season because of a knee injury, and he told The Hollywood Reporter the setback changed how he trains and how urgently he treats the time he has left on the court. He said he doesn't dwell on his basketball mortality, but the injury sharpened his intentionality and pushed him to work smarter at 38 than he did five years ago.Ayesha said she reminds him of his ceiling almost every week. "I just know you have one more chip in you," she told the outlet, adding that she repeats the line to him often. Stephen backed that up by saying he still wants to be the hardest worker in any room he walks into, whatever the season brings.Ayesha is headlining Second Chance Holiday alongside Matthew Daddario for the Roku Channel, playing a dating expert convinced she is cursed to get dumped every holiday season until an old flame resurfaces. It marks her first time topping a film call sheet after supporting roles in Irish Wish, Joy to the World and A Black Lady Sketch Show, plus a voice role in this year's animated hit GOAT."It's an honor to be part of Second Chance Holiday," Curry said in a statement announcing the project. Stephen, meanwhile, has built out Unanimous Media into a full production company under a Comcast NBCUniversal deal, with GOAT grossing close to $200 million and the MLK documentary short The Baddest Speechwriter of All picked up by Netflix after winning at Sundance. The duo is growing in the entertainment industry.Curry became the fourth NBA player to win the Muhammad Ali Sports Humanitarian Award, following Chris Paul, Kevin Durant and Jrue Holiday, with Ayesha by his side at New York's David H. Koch Theater on July 15. She marked the moment on Instagram with a simple message: "So proud of my baby, always."Accepting the award from Lindsey Vonn, Curry credited Oakland's role in the foundation's growth. "It's about what happens when a community shows up for its kids," he said, pointing to the city where the Warriors won their first three titles together. Eat. Learn. Play., which he and Ayesha launched in 2019, has since expanded access to meals, education and recreation programs for local youth, and the recognition keeps coming: People Magazine reports the couple will join singer John Mayer and actor Michael J. Fox as Philanthropists of the Year at next week's Social Impact Summit.None of it settles the bigger basketball question hanging over Curry's summer. He's already made a personal pitch to free agent LeBron James about joining the Warriors, and his own contract situation, one year remaining, remains unresolved heading into training camp.
Article Summary: Ayesha curry says she reminds stephen of his ceiling almost every week. The golden state warriors star won the muhammad ali sports humanitarian award at the 2026 espys. He and ayesha are set to be named philanthropists of the year at the hollywood reporter's social impact summit.

Article Title: Who is Yaxel Lendeborg? All about Summer League championship game MVP as Warriors beat Grizzlies
Article Text:
Yaxel Lendeborg. Image via: Mike Kirschbaum/NBAE via Getty Images

Who is Warriors star Yaxel Lendeborg?

Who are Yaxel Lendeborg's parents?

Yaxel Lendeborg's mom has a huge role in his career

axel Lendeborg and his mom Yissel Raposo. Image via: Yaxel Lendeborg/X

Inside Yaxel Lendeborg's relationship with his father

Yaxel Lendeborg earned Summer League championship game MVP honors after leading the Golden State Warriors past the Memphis Grizzlies 94-90 in the 2026 NBA Summer League title game on Sunday in Las Vegas.The Warriors' first-round pick posted 21 points, 10 rebounds, two assists and two steals, capping a breakout six-game run in Las Vegas that announced him as one of the league's most promising rookies.Golden State trailed by 10 entering the fourth quarter before Lendeborg's late surge sealed the franchise's first Summer League title since 2013.Lendeborg was born in Puerto Rico and starred at the University of Michigan before Golden State selected him No. 11 overall in the 2026 NBA Draft. He arrived at the Summer League title game fresh off a Michigan national championship, and he carried that momentum into Las Vegas, averaging 14.8 points, 7.2 rebounds and 4.0 assists across six appearances.In the championship game itself, Lendeborg shot 9 of 18 from the field and hit a go-ahead three-pointer with just over two minutes remaining, giving the Warriors their first lead since the middle of the third quarter.He followed it with a midrange pull-up that forced a Memphis timeout, then grabbed a critical rebound after the Grizzlies missed a potential go-ahead three in the closing seconds. Deivon Smith added 21 points off the bench, while Memphis got 19 points apiece from Cameron Boozer and Cedric Coward in the loss.Basketball runs in Lendeborg's family. His mother, Yissel Raposo, and father, Okary Lendeborg, both played for the Dominican Republic national basketball team, and Yissel also competed for the country's national volleyball team. She was playing both sports collegiately at the American University of Puerto Rico when Yaxel was born, while his father played professionally overseas. Lendeborg has credited his parents directly for his athletic gifts, telling MLive.com he has natural ability that just comes from my parents.Yissel's influence on her son's path has been constant and, at times, decisive. She bought him NBA gear as a child and recalled him always answering that he wanted to reach the league.When academic struggles made him ineligible to play basketball for two years in high school, Yissel intervened during his senior year, arranging his transfer to a program in Camden, New Jersey, and later using her connections from her playing days in the Dominican system to get him into a camp in New York that led to a scholarship offer from Arizona Western College. Lendeborg has said his mother essentially put him on a plane to Arizona despite his own hesitation.In a February 2026 essay for The Players' Tribune, Lendeborg wrote about his mother's cancer diagnosis and his drive to make her proud, saying he wanted to cut down the nets at Michigan's national championship run most of all for her. He was later seen in tears embracing her after Golden State drafted him, telling ESPN's Lisa Salters he was glad to share that moment with her.Lendeborg has been more guarded about his relationship with his father, describing tension that grew more hostile at points during his upbringing. He has written that his mother tried to help the two find common ground without much success (via PEOPLE ).He has also spoken about leaning on his grandfather, Yissel's father, as an early father figure, and about struggling with motivation after his grandfather's death before his mother helped redirect him toward basketball and school.
Article Summary: Yaxel lendeborg led the golden state warriors past the memphis grizzlies 94-90 in the 2026 nba summer league title game on sunday. He posted 21 points, 10 rebounds, two assists and two steals, capping a breakout six-game run in las vegas that announced him as one of the league's most promising rookies.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Nico Williams: "I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother. Because she once escaped death and…" - an emotional tribute to his mother, the extraordinary journey behind h
Article Text:
Nico Williams (Image: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Nico Williams

"I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother. Because she once escaped death and crossed the Sahara Desert from Ghana to Spain to become who I am today. I gave the medal to my mother because she deserves it more than anyone else"

The moment behind the quote by Nico William

What the comparison is actually saying

Why he gave the medal away

Why sacrifice so often stays invisible behind success

Why this quote still resonates beyond football

A winner's medal usually gets read as proof of what an athlete just achieved on the pitch. Nico Williams, the Spanish footballer, read his differently. "I run very fast, but I can't run as fast as my mother," he said. "Because she once escaped death and crossed the Sahara Desert from Ghana to Spain to become who I am today. I gave the medal to my mother because she deserves it more than anyone else." A medal earned in ninety minutes was, in his own telling, connected to a much longer and harder journey that began decades earlier, long before he was born, on a route that plenty of people crossing it did not survive. His gesture transformed a personal sporting achievement into a powerful tribute to sacrifice, resilience and the countless struggles his family overcame before reaching a safer future. The story is a poignant reminder that behind many public successes lie unseen sacrifices, with achievements often built upon the courage and determination of those who came before us.Williams said this after Spain's Euro 2024 victory over England in July 2024, a tournament in which he scored the opening goal of the final and was named one of the standout performers of the whole competition. He was born in Pamplona, Spain, to Ghanaian parents, and his older brother, Iñaki Williams, plays international football for Ghana rather than Spain.The family's migration story, crossing the Sahara before eventually settling in Spain, sits directly behind both brothers' careers.Williams is known specifically for his speed on the pitch, which makes the comparison land harder than it otherwise might. He is not measuring his mother's journey against his own athletic pace literally. He is arguing that the courage and endurance it took her to survive that crossing outweighs anything he has done as a professional footballer, however fast he happens to be able to run.Plenty of athletes dedicate a win to their families in general terms. Williams did something more specific, treating the medal as something connected directly to his mother's own sacrifice rather than solely to his own performance. In his framing, the achievement had more than one author, his training and effort on one side, and the earlier decisions and hardship that made his life in Spain possible on the other.A medal is visible. A goal gets replayed endlessly. The decisions and hardship that came years earlier, the ones that actually made the achievement possible in the first place, rarely get the same attention. Williams' quote pulls that hidden part of the story directly into view, refusing to let his own success stand apart from what his parents went through to give him the chance at it. It serves as a reminder that behind many celebrated sporting moments lies a family's quiet sacrifice, years of persistence, financial struggles and unwavering belief that often remain unseen long after the applause fades.Plenty of people carry family histories shaped by migration and hardship they did not personally experience but still benefit from directly. Williams' quote gives that quiet inheritance a specific, public voice, treating his own visible success as something built on a far less visible journey that came before it.
Article Summary: Spanish footballer nico williams gave away his euro 2024 medal to his mother. He was born in pamplona, spain, to ghanaian parents. His older brother, iñaki williams, plays international football for ghana. The family's migration story, crossing the sahara, sits directly behind both brothers' careers.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal: "My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to..." - a powerful lesson on perspective, family sacrifice and why football is not the hardest battle his fa
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal

Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal

"My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to bring food home. That's real pressure. All I have to do is play football"

The moment behind Lamine Yamal’s quote

The family story behind the words

What the comparison is actually doing

Why this reflects more than modesty

Other things Lamine Yamal has said

"It moves me to see my brother so happy, to see my mother living the life she's always dreamed of, and my friends, too. It's a child's greatest dream."

"I'm in love with my little brother. I love him like a son."

Why this still resonates beyond football

Pressure gets discussed constantly in football, usually about missed chances and expectant crowds. Lamine Yamal, Spain's teenage forward, measured his own pressure against something else entirely. "My mother had me when she was 16, that's real pressure," he said. "My father had to figure out life, going out to the street to pick things up. All I have to do is play football." Asked to explain the weight of carrying a national team, he pointed the question straight back at his own family, refusing to let his own circumstances, remarkable as they already were at nineteen, be treated as the hardest thing anyone in his family had actually lived through, given everything his parents had gone through years before he ever kicked a ball professionally. His perspective is a reminder that gratitude and perspective can reshape how we view our own challenges, making even the biggest stages seem smaller when compared with the sacrifices that made them possible.Yamal said this in the build-up to Spain's run at the 2026 World Cup, a tournament Spain went on to win, beating Argentina 1-0 in the final at MetLife Stadium on 19 July, only days after Yamal turned nineteen. Pushed on the pressure of leading the team, he redirected the question entirely: "Don't paint me as if what I'm experiencing is the real pressure.There are people who have endured far more." His response reflected a maturity beyond his age, showing that he viewed football success through the lens of perspective, gratitude and respect for those facing greater struggles.Accordinf to reports, his mother, Sheila Ebana, moved to Catalonia from Bata, Equatorial Guinea, and had Yamal at sixteen. His father, Mounir Nasraoui, arrived in Spain from Morocco as a young child and later worked scrap collection and other temporary jobs to get by. The couple separated when Yamal was around three, and he grew up largely in Rocafonda, a working-class neighbourhood in Mataró, in a small flat where the kitchen and bedroom were effectively one room.Yamal is not describing professional football as easy. Reaching the top of the sport by nineteen required years of relentless training and competing against far older, more experienced players. What he is doing is placing that effort next to a different kind of pressure entirely, the pressure of becoming a parent at sixteen, or of scraping together enough money to keep a family fed. Next to that, he treats his own job, difficult as it genuinely is, as something closer to a privilege than a burden.Public success can easily narrow someone's view until their own difficulties feel like the only ones that matter. Yamal's comment does the opposite. Despite being one of the most closely watched young players in the world, he places his own challenge inside the context of what his parents actually went through to give him the chance at it in the first place.Most people compare their own struggles to someone else's at some point, and it rarely makes the struggle disappear. What it can do is add context. Yamal's own difficulty, performing under enormous public attention at nineteen, is genuinely real. He has simply chosen to measure it against something he considers larger, and decided his own job looks smaller by comparison.
Article Summary: Lamine yamal, spain's teenage forward, measured his own pressure against something else entirely. Asked to explain the weight of carrying a national team, he pointed the question straight back at his own family. His response reflected a maturity beyond his age, showing that he viewed football success through the lens of perspective.

Kapil Pokhariya

Event:

CWG Record:

Article Title: Quote of the day by David Beckham: "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to…" - a powerful lesson on hard work, discipline and why talent alone is never
Article Text:
David Beckham

Quote of the day by David Beckham

"I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more"

What is the meaning behind David Beckham’s quote

Why talent alone rarely gets someone all the way there

Why setbacks tested this philosophy directly

Why consistency matters more than occasional motivation

Other famous quotes by David Beckham

"The secret to my success is practice!"

"The secret of the team's success is easy, unity and hard work."

"You will go through tough times, it's about getting through them."

"I don't have time for hobbies. At the end of the day, I treat my job as a hobby. It's something I love doing."

Why it still feels relevant today

People tend to admire success from a safe distance, seeing the trophy without ever seeing the years that produced it. David Beckham summed up his own version of that gap plainly. "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more," he said. The repetition is not accidental. He is describing something continuous, not a single burst of effort that happens once and then stops. Coming from a player often remembered for his technique on free kicks and crosses, the line carries the weight of someone who actually built that skill through repetition, rather than someone simply repeating a motivational slogan he never had to test himself. His journey from a young academy player to a global football icon reflects the discipline, patience and relentless commitment required to turn natural ability into lasting excellence on the biggest stages. The quote serves as a timeless reminder that extraordinary achievements are rarely the result of talent alone, but of consistent effort sustained long after the initial excitement has faded.The phrase "anything special in life" is deliberately broad, applying just as easily to a career in business or medicine as it does to professional sport. The answer he gives is repeated three times for a reason, work, work, work some more, because Beckham is not describing hard work as an occasional event.He is describing it as the actual, unglamorous rhythm behind lasting achievement.Natural ability offers a real head start, but plenty of gifted people never fully develop it, while plenty of less naturally talented people outwork their way past them over time. Former teammates and coaches have repeatedly described Beckham staying behind after regular training to practise free kicks and crosses long after everyone else had gone home, which is really the practical version of the quote in action.Beckham faced serious public criticism at points in his career, most famously after being sent off during England's 1998 World Cup exit to Argentina. He later described that period bluntly. "I have nightmares about France 98," he said. "It was humiliating. It will always be with me." Rather than letting that moment define the rest of his career, he kept working and went on to become one of England's most recognised footballers, a direct example of the same principle behind today's quote.Motivation naturally rises and falls, strong some days and completely absent on others. Discipline is what keeps someone working through the days motivation does not show up, which is really the difference between an ambition that stays an idea and one that eventually becomes a career.Modern culture often makes success look instant, a single viral moment or a sudden breakthrough. Beckham's own career is a reminder that most of what actually produces lasting results happens quietly, well before anyone else is paying attention, repeated on ordinary days long after the initial excitement of starting has worn off.
Article Summary: "i have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to work, work, and then work some more" - david beckham. The quote serves as a timeless reminder that extraordinary achievements are rarely the result of talent alone. Discipline is what keeps someone working through the days motivation does not show up.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El rubius was eliminated from mrbeast's $1 million challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

RCB register their second win

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal challengers bengaluru (rcb) beat chennai super kings (csk) in ipl 2026 encounter at the m chinnaswamy stadium on sunday. Rcb register their second win over csk in the ipl. Tim david, rajat patidar and devdutt padikkal all scored half-centuries for rcb. Bhuvneshwar kumar took 3-41 to help rcb reach 250/3.
